channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: GB's Klugman beaten in Nottingham second round
item.description: British teenager Hannah Klugman says she feels "very different" after a week practising with "some great players" following her second-round Nottingham Open defeat.

item.title: 'I never imagined something like this' - Nadal reflects on 2008 Wimbledon win
item.description: Rafael Nadal recalls his seismic five-set victory over Roger Federer in the 2008 Wimbledon final, arguably the greatest men's match ever played.

item.title: Best shots as GB's Fery reaches Queen's quarter-finals
item.description: Watch the best shots as Great Britain's Arthur Ferry reaches his first ATP Tour quarter-final with a straight-set win over French veteran Adrian Mannarino at Queen's.

item.title: Fery makes Queen's quarter-finals with 'best result of career'
item.description: British number five Arthur Fery produces a superb display to beat Adrian Mannarino and keep home hopes alive in the HSBC Championships at Queen's.

item.title: Evans misses out on Wimbledon singles wildcard
item.description: British tennis stalwart Dan Evans will have to go through qualifying to make the main draw of the men's singles at Wimbledon after missing out on a wildcard.

item.title: Best shots as GB's Norrie beaten by Davidovich Fokina
item.description: Watch the best shots as Britain's Cameron Norrie falls to Alejandro Davidovich Fokina in the Round of 32 at Queen's.

item.title: Norrie beaten at Queen's on injury return
item.description: Cameron Norrie is beaten by Alejandro Davidovich Fokina at Queen's in the Briton's first singles match since suffering a rib injury in May.

item.title: Moutet swears seven times in live BBC interview
item.description: Corentin Moutet says he "hopes you guys didn't get offended" after swearing seven times in a live BBC television interview at Queen's.

item.title: Williams sisters receive Wimbledon doubles wildcard
item.description: Serena Williams is given a wildcard to play alongside older sister Venus in the Wimbledon women's doubles.

item.title: Evans questions lack of 'classy gesture' after Queen's wildcard decision
item.description: Britain's Dan Evans says it would have been "a classy gesture" to give him a Queen's wildcard in his penultimate tournament before retirement.

item.title: How to follow the men's tournament at Queen's on the BBC
item.description: How to follow the men's HSBC Championships at Queen's live on the BBC.

item.title: Vekic beats Raducanu in straight sets to win Queen's final
item.description: Watch highlights as Donna Vekic defeats Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6 (8-6) to win the women's singles final at Queens.

item.title: Raducanu thankful for 'incredible support' after Queen's final loss
item.description: Emma Raducanu says she "felt her best" being able to play in her home city, despite the Briton losing the Queen's Club Championship women's singles final to Donna Vekic.

item.title: Vekic grateful for Queen's Club opportunity after title win
item.description: Donna Vekic praises those who made women playing at Queen's Club possible, after becoming the competition's second ever Women's singles champion following a straight sets win over Emma Raducanu in the final.

item.title: Raducanu cruises past Jovic to reach Queen's final
item.description: Britain's Emma Raducanu reaches her first ever grass court final at Queen's with a dominant 6-2, 6-2 victory against American Iva Jovic.

item.title: Raducanu overcomes 'tough moments' to reach Queen's final
item.description: Britain's Emma Raducanu battled through a quarter-final and semi-final match in one day to secure her spot in the Queen's final, defeating Uzbekistan's Kamilla Rakhimova and USA's Iva Jovic.

item.title: Spain's new Rafa eyes grass-court success in year of firsts
item.description: It has been a year of firsts for Spain's breakout teenage star Rafael Jodar - and he will hit another milestone when he makes his Wimbledon debut next week.

item.title: How are Wimbledon wildcards decided?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how wildcard entries are decided at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'The new Emma' - positives Raducanu can take from Queen's to Wimbledon
item.description: Emma Raducanu falls agonisingly short at Queen's after losing in Sunday's final - but there are huge positives to take with Wimbledon just around the corner.

item.title: Bridesmaid duties await Nicholls after Queen's joy
item.description: Tennis star Olivia Nicholls will be playing a key role at her doubles partner's wedding.

item.title: GB's Jones misses match points in Nottingham loss
item.description: Britain's Francesca Jones misses two match points in a narrow defeat by Talia Gibson in the Nottingham Open first round.

item.title: Raducanu loses Queen's final as trophy wait continues
item.description: Emma Raducanu misses the chance to win a first title since her 2021 US Open triumph as she is beaten in the Queen's final by Croatia's Donna Vekic.

item.title: Boulter stuns Rybakina at Queen's for biggest career win
item.description: An inspired Katie Boulter celebrates the biggest win of her career by ranking as she beats world number two Elena Rybakina to reach the Queen's semi-finals.

item.title: Raducanu overcomes injury scare to reach Queen's semis
item.description: Britain's Emma Raducanu overcomes an injury scare to beat Uzbekistan's Kamilla Rakhimova 6-3, 7-5 and advance to the semi-finals of the Queen's Club Championship.

item.title: Raducanu 'really proud' to overcome injury scare in Queen's quarter-final
item.description: Britain's Emma Raducanu reacts to her win against Uzbekistan's Kamilla Rakhimova as she advances to the semi-finals of Queen's.

item.title: Best shots as Boulter stuns Rybakina
item.description: Watch the best shots as Britain's Katie Boulter stuns top seed Elena Rybakina to book her Queen's semi-final spot.

item.title: 'This win goes to the crowd' - Boulter on victory over Rybakina
item.description: Britain's Katie Boulter dedicates her stunning upset of world number two Elena Rybakina to the crowd who stayed late to witness her quarter-final battle.

item.title: Raducanu beats Cirstea to book Queen's quarter-final spot
item.description: Britain's Emma Raducanu books her place in the quarter-finals of the Queen's Club Championship with a dominant straight-set win over Romania's Sorana Cirstea.

item.title: Balancing babies and breaks - how tennis protects player rankings
item.description: BBC Sport's Annabel Croft and Naomi Broady discuss how the WTA reformed its rulebook, ensuring players no longer have to choose between family planning and their professional tennis careers.

item.title: Williams' doubles partner retires in second set of singles match after fall
item.description: Victoria Mboko retires in the second set of her singles match against Karolina Pliskova after a heavy fall. The Canadian is scheduled to play with Serena Williams in doubles on Thursday.

item.title: The cost of perfection - Croft on the sacrifices of tennis players
item.description: BBC Sport's Annabel Croft says it is "impossible for tennis players to have a normal social life" because viewing peers as rivals restricts friendships and affects general happiness. Croft explains how tennis players often discover themselves after retirement, as they can figure out who they are away from court.

item.title: Williams makes winning return at Queen's
item.description: Serena Williams and Victoria Mboko defeat third seeds Nicole Melichar-Martinez and Erin Routliffe to reach the second round at Queen's. The unseeded pair came through a tie-break in the opening set before racing away with the second set to win 7-6 (7-2) 6-2.

item.title: 'You can do anything at any age' - Williams on Queen's comeback
item.description: Serena Williams makes a triumphant return to professional tennis at the Queen's Club, securing a first-round doubles victory alongside Canada's Victoria Mboko. Playing on the Andy Murray Arena, the 44-year-old American icon inspired the crowd by declaring "you can do anything at any age" following their victory.

item.title: Players welcome 'genuine step forward' as Wimbledon increases prize money by 20%
item.description: Prize money at this year’s Wimbledon will increase by 20% to £64.2m, with singles’ champions each taking home £3.6m.

item.title: Former British number one Evans to retire after Wimbledon
item.description: Former British number one Dan Evans is to retire from tennis after this year's Wimbledon.

item.title: Williams' Queen's run over as injured Mboko withdraws
item.description: Serena Williams' doubles campaign at Queen's is curtailed as her playing partner Victoria Mboko withdraws with a left knee injury.

item.title: GB to face Czech Republic in BJK Cup quarter-final
item.description: Great Britain are drawn against the Czech Republic in the quarter-final stage of this year's Billie Jean King Cup.

item.title: Kyrgios' return to action ends in second round
item.description: Australian Nick Kyrgios' return to action at the Stuttgart Open ends with a second-round loss to Japanese qualifier Sho Shimabukuro.

item.title: 'Maybe a C-minus?' - how good was Williams' winning return?
item.description: Serena Williams rolls back the years at Queen's with a winning performance on her comeback to tennis after 1,375 days away from the sport.

item.title: Draper delays comeback and misses Queen's
item.description: British number three Jack Draper says he hopes to make his return from a knee injury at the Eastbourne Open this month.

item.title: How Williams' British hitting partner kept one of tennis' biggest secrets
item.description: The British hitting partner of Serena Williams tells BBC Sport how he became involved in one of tennis' closest-guarded secrets.

item.title: Five reasons why this French Open was a classic - pick your favourite moment
item.description: Why a series of shocks, dramas and fairytale runs made this year's French Open a instant classic.

item.title: 'I want to thank myself' - teenager Andreeva's journey to Grand Slam glory
item.description: Mirra Andreeva thanks her coach, her team and most importantly herself as she claims her first Grand Slam trophy at the French Open.

item.title: Will Sabalenka and Osaka open door for women's night sessions?
item.description: It may only have lasted 87 minutes, but Aryna Sabalenka's match against Naomi Osaka brought an X-factor to a very rare French Open women's night-session match.

item.title: Why beating Djokovic is coming of age moment for Fonseca
item.description: Why Joao Fonseca's five-set French Open victory over the legendary Novak Djokovic shows the 19-year-old has arrived at the top of tennis.

item.title: Travelling solo and sharing rooms - what is life like on tennis’ lower rung?
item.description: Sleeping in a transit van is one of the money-saving tricks used by those on the lower rung of the tennis circuit.

item.title: 'I broke the curse' - the fall and rise of a teenage Grand Slam champion
item.description: When 19-year-old Bianca Andreescu stunned Serena Williams to win the US Open it was unthinkable it would be more than 2,300 days before her next triumph.

item.title: Who has won tennis' 'Battle of the Sexes'?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team details the history of 'Battle of the Sexes' matches in tennis

item.title: What is US Open prize money?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how much prize money is on offer at the US Open.

item.title: Why is there a pineapple on Wimbledon's men's trophy?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at why there is a pineapple on the top of the Wimbledon trophy for the winner of the men's singles.

item.title: Why were there no line judges at Wimbledon 2025?
item.description: Wimbledon are relying on electronic line calling to judge whether a ball is in or out.
